column1: Integrate the Platonic solids and their elemental correspondences.
Clearly distinguish between the classical Western elements (Earth, Water, Air, Fire, Aether) and their philosophical sources (Plato, Aristotle, Neoplatonism, etc.).
Include other Western esoteric systems (e.g., Hermeticism, Kabbalah, Neoplatonism) and their elemental or energy center analogs.
Provide clear citations for each association.

Below is an expanded and more accurate comparative table, followed by an explanation of the Platonic solids and their chakra associations, and a summary of key Western philosophical and esoteric systems.
Expanded Comparative Table: Elemental and Chakra-like Systems
Platonic Solids, Elements, and Chakra Correspondences
Plato described five perfect solids, each associated with a classical element and, in modern esoteric systems, mapped to chakras as follows:
The Dodecahedron is often linked with Aether (quintessence) and associated with the higher chakras (Throat, Third Eye, Crown), reflecting the transcendental or cosmic aspect of these centers24.
